title: Broken Stack Overflow Handling: Misbalanced Elements

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers representing elements, your task is to check if a subarray has elements that perfectly match a balanced sequence of parentheses. This means every opening parenthesis has a corresponding closing parenthesis and vice versa. However, there's a bug in the current implementation where it incorrectly handles stack overflows. Your task is to fix this bug and provide the correct implementation of this function.
statement.inputFormat: The input will contain an array of integers. Each integer represents a character from 0-9. A 0 represents an opening parenthesis, and a 1 represents a closing parenthesis.
statement.outputFormat: Your function should return true if a balanced subarray exists, false otherwise.

editorial.title: Broken Stack Overflow Handling: Misbalanced Elements

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { int balance = 0; for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { if (nums[i] == 0) balance++; else if (balance > 0 && nums[i] == 1) balance--; else { balance = nums[i]; } return balance > 0; } } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] nums) { int balance = 0; for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { if (nums[i] == 0) balance++; else if (balance > 0 && nums[i] == 1) balance--; else { balance = nums[i]; } return balance > 0; } } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: balance = 0 for i in nums: if i == 0: balance += 1 elif balance > 0 and i == 1: balance -= 1 else: balance = i return balance > 0
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var solve = function(nums) { let balance = 0; for (let i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { if (nums[i] == 0) balance++; else if (balance > 0 && nums[i] == 1) balance--; else { balance = nums[i]; } return balance > 0; } };

solutions.python: class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: stack = [] for i in range(len(nums)): if nums[i] == 0: stack.append(0) elif nums[i] == 1: if not stack: return False stack.pop() return len(stack) == 0
solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { stack<int> s; for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { if (nums[i] == 0) s.push(0); else if (nums[i] == 1 && s.empty()) return false; else s.pop(); } return s.empty(); } };
